\section{Theory Model Equilibrium Characterization\label{sec:Working}}

\subsection{Characterizing the R\&D Allocation for Upstream Firms}

Copying from the main text, we have the following optimization problem
\[
\max_{\left\{ I_{f,c},I_{f,j,c}\right\} _{j,c}}\sum_{m}q_{f,m}\left(p_{f.m}-\frac{\bar{c}_{f,m}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j_{m}}^{\varrho}}\right)-\sum_{c}r_{c}^{\zeta}\left(I_{f,c}+\sum_{j}I_{f,j,c},\right)
\]
subject to the following aggregation
\[
I_{f}=\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{f,c}\zeta_{f,c}\right]^{\varsigma}\right)^{1/\varsigma}\text{ and \ensuremath{I_{f,j}}=\ensuremath{\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{f,j}\zeta_{f,j,c}\right]^{\zeta}\right)^{1/\varsigma}}}.
\]
Taking first-order conditions, I have 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\left\{ I_{f,c}\right\} :r_{c}^{\zeta}= & \rho I_{f}^{-\rho-1}\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{f,c}\zeta_{f,c}\right]^{\varsigma}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left[I_{f,c}\zeta_{f,c}\right]^{\varsigma-1}\zeta_{f,c}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\sum_{m}q_{f,m}\bar{c}_{f,m}\\
1= & \rho I_{f}^{-\rho-1}\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{f,c}\zeta_{f,c}\right]^{\varsigma}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left(I_{f,c}\right)^{\zeta-1}\left[\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
\left\{ I_{f,j,c}\right\} :r_{c}^{\zeta}= & \varrho I_{f,j}^{-\varrho-1}\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{f,j,c}\zeta_{f,j,c}\right]^{\zeta}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left[I_{f,j,c}\zeta_{f,j,c}\right]^{\varsigma-1}\zeta_{f,j,c}I_{f}^{-\rho}\sum_{m}\mathbb{1}\left(j_{m}=j\right)q_{f,m}\bar{c}_{f,m}\\
1= & \varrho I_{f}^{-\varrho-1}\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{f,j,c}\zeta_{f,j,c}\right]^{\zeta}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left(I_{f,j,c}\right)^{\zeta-1}\left[\zeta_{f,j,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}I_{f}^{-\rho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Where we defined
\[
\bar{q}_{f,j}=\sum_{m}\mathbb{1}\left\{ j_{m}=j\right\} q_{f,m}\text{ and }\bar{c}_{f,j}=\sum_{m}\mathbb{1}\left\{ j_{m}=j\right\} \frac{q_{f,m}}{\bar{q}_{f,j}}c_{f,m}
\]
such that $\sum_{m}\mathbb{1}\left(j_{m}=j\right)q_{f,m}\bar{c}_{f,m}=\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}$.
Next, I divide FOCs for any two countries to get 
\begin{equation}
\frac{\left[\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}I_{f,c}^{\zeta-1}}{\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}I_{f,c'}^{\zeta-1}}=1\implies\left[\frac{\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}}{\zeta_{f,c'}\lambda_{c'}^{-1}}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}=\frac{I_{f,c}}{I_{f,c'}}.\label{eq: Relative Country Allocation}
\end{equation}
Now I can write aggregate R\&D in terms of country-specific R\&D:
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}I_{f}^{\zeta}= & \sum_{c'}\left[I_{f,c'}\zeta_{f,c'}\right]^{\varsigma}\\
= & I_{f,c}^{\zeta}\sum_{c'}\left[\frac{I_{f,c'}}{I_{f,c}}\zeta_{f,c'}\right]^{\varsigma}\\
= & I_{f,c}^{\zeta}\sum_{c'}\left[\left[\frac{\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-1}}{\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\zeta_{f,c'}\right]^{\varsigma}\\
= & \frac{I_{f,c}^{\zeta}}{\left(\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma^{2}}{1-\varsigma}}}\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Rearranging terms, this gives 
\[
I_{f,c}\zeta_{f,c}=\frac{\left(\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-\varsigma}\right)^{\frac{1}{1-\varsigma}}}{\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varsigma}}}I_{f}
\]
Now, this let's us write the FOC for $\left\{ I_{f,c}\right\} $
as 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}1= & \rho I_{f}^{-\rho-1}\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{f,c}\zeta_{f,c}\right]^{\varsigma}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left(I_{f,c}\right)^{\zeta-1}\left[\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
= & \rho I_{f}^{-\rho-1}\left(\frac{I_{f,c}^{\zeta}}{\left(\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma^{2}}{1-\varsigma}}}\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left(I_{f,c}\right)^{\zeta-1}\left[\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
= & \rho I_{f}^{-\rho-1}I_{f,c}^{\zeta\left(1/\varsigma-1\right)+\left(\varsigma+1\right)}\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}\left(\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right)^{-\frac{\varsigma^{2}}{1-\varsigma}\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}+\varsigma}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
= & \rho I_{f}^{-\rho-1}\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
I_{f}^{1+\rho}= & \rho\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Repeating similar steps with the other first-order condition, one
would get 
\[
I_{f,j}^{1+\varrho}=\varrho\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,j,c}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}I_{f}^{-\rho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}.
\]
Now we define 
\[
\bar{\zeta}_{f}=\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}\text{ and }\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}=\left(\sum_{c}\left[\zeta_{f,j,c}r_{c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}.
\]
which let's us simply write
\begin{equation}
I_{f}^{1+\rho}=\rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\text{ and }I_{f,j}^{1+\varrho}=\varrho I_{f}^{-\rho}\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}.\label{eq: I_f and I_fj relation}
\end{equation}
Plugging in the later to the former, we get 
\begin{equation}
\begin{alignedat}{1}I_{f}^{1+\rho}= & \rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
= & \rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}\left(I_{f}^{-\rho}\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
\left(I_{f}\right)^{1+\rho-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\varrho}}= & \rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}\left(\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
I_{f}= & \left(\rho\varrho^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}}\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}\left(\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\varrho}{1+\rho+\varrho}}
\end{alignedat}
\label{eq: I_f final allocation}
\end{equation}
Comparing the relative allocations across generic and specialized
R\&D using (\ref{eq: I_f and I_fj relation}), we have:
\[
\frac{I_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}=\frac{\rho}{\varrho}\sum_{j}\frac{I_{f,j}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}
\]
Again using (\ref{eq: I_f and I_fj relation}), we can write the allocation
across specialized R\&D recipients as 

\[
\frac{I_{f,j}}{\sum I_{f,j}}=\frac{\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho}}}{\sum_{j}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho}}}
\]
And finally comparing the relative allocations across locations using
\ref{eq: Relative Country Allocation}, we have:

\[
\frac{I_{f,c}}{\sum_{c}I_{f,c}}=\frac{\left(\zeta_{f,c}\lambda_{f,c}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}}{\sum\left(\zeta_{f,c}\lambda_{f,c}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}}\text{ and }\frac{I_{f,j,c}}{\sum_{c}I_{f,j,c}}=\frac{\left(\zeta_{f,j,c}\lambda_{f,c'}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}}{\sum\left(\zeta_{f,j,c}\lambda_{f,c'}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}}.
\]
Taking the results together, we have the components of Proposition
\ref{prop: Suppliers R=000026D Allocation}. 

Using (\ref{eq: I_f and I_fj relation}) and (\ref{eq: I_f final allocation}),
we can characterize the prices as follows: 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}p_{f,j,k}= & \mu c_{f,j,k}I_{f}^{-\rho}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\\
= & \mu c_{f,j,k}I_{f}^{-\rho}\left(I_{f}^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\varrho}}\left(\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}.\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho}}\right)^{-\varrho}\\
= & \mu c_{f,j,k}I_{f}^{-\rho\left(1+\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}\right)}\left(\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}.\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}}\\
= & \mu c_{f,j,k}I_{f}^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\varrho}}\left(\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}.\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}}\\
= & \mu c_{f,j,k}\left(\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}.\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}}\left(\rho\varrho^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}}\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}\left(\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho+\varrho}}\\
= & \mu c_{f,j,k}\left(\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}.\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho}}\left(\sum_{j}\frac{\rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho+\varrho}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Now, if we wish to concentrate prices outside of quantities, let's
write 
\[
\bar{q}_{f,j}=x_{f,j}p_{f,j}^{-\theta}=\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\varrho}}\right)^{-\theta}
\]
and write new expressions for $I_{f}$ and $I_{f,j}$:
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}I_{f}^{1+\rho}= & \rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
= & \rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\varrho}}\right)^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}\\
I_{f}^{1+\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}= & \rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}
\end{alignedat}
\]
and 
\[
I_{f,j}^{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}=\varrho I_{f}^{-\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{\left(1-\theta\right)}.
\]
We can follow the same derivation as above to deliver 
\[
I_{f}=\left(\rho\varrho^{\frac{-\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}\left(\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}^{-\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}
\]
and likewise 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}p_{f,j}= & \mu_{f,j}c_{f,j}\left(\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\frac{\rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\varrho\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}\end{alignedat}
\]


\subsection{Characterizing the R\&D Allocation for Downstream Firms}

I think we estimate the fixed effects of parts as like the cost components.
Something like that. 

\[
\max_{p_{j},\{I_{j,A,c},I_{j,I,c}\}_{c}}D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\left(p_{j}-\left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}}\right)-\sum_{c\in C_{j}}r_{c}^{\zeta}\Bigl(I_{j,A,c}+\sum_{p}I_{j,p,c}\Bigr)
\]
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\left\{ I_{j,p,c}\right\} :r_{c}^{\zeta}=\left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}-1}\left(\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho-1} & c_{j,p}\rho I_{j,p}^{-\rho-1}\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{j,p,c}\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}\right]^{\varsigma}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left[I_{j,p,c}\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}\right]^{\varsigma-1}\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\\
1=\left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}-1}\left(\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho-1} & c_{j,p}\rho I_{j,p}^{-\rho-1}\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{j,p,c}\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}\right]^{\varsigma}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left(I_{j,p,c}\right)^{\zeta-1}\left[\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}q_{j}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Next, we divide FOCs for two countries to get 
\[
\frac{\left[\zeta_{j,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{j,c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}I_{j,c}^{\zeta-1}}{\left[\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{j,c'}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}I_{j,c'}^{\zeta-1}}=1\implies\left[\frac{\zeta_{j,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{j,c}^{-1}}{\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{j,c'}^{-1}}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}=\frac{I_{f,c}}{I_{f,c'}}.
\]
Now we can write aggregate R\&D in terms of country-specific R\&D:
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}I_{j}^{\zeta}= & \sum_{c'}\left[I_{j,c'}\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}\right]^{\varsigma}\\
= & I_{j,c}^{\zeta}\sum_{c'}\left[\frac{I_{j,c'}}{I_{j,c}}\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}\right]^{\varsigma}\\
= & I_{j,c}^{\zeta}\sum_{c'}\left[\left[\frac{\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{j,c'}^{-1}}{\zeta_{j,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{j,c}^{-1}}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}\right]^{\varsigma}\\
= & \frac{I_{j,c}^{\zeta}}{\left(\zeta_{j,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{j,c}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma^{2}}{1-\varsigma}}}\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}r_{j,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Which one can plug back into the FOC: 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}1= & \left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}-1}\left(\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho-1}c_{j,p}\rho I_{j,p}^{-\rho-1}\left(\sum_{c}\left[I_{j,p,c}\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}\right]^{\varsigma}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left(I_{j,p,c}\right)^{\zeta-1}\left[\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}\lambda_{f,c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}q_{j}\\
= & \left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}-1}\left(\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho-1}\rho I_{j,p}^{-\rho-1}\left(\frac{I_{j,c}^{\zeta}}{\left(\zeta_{j,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}\lambda_{j,c}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma^{2}}{1-\varsigma}}}\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}\lambda_{j,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{1/\varsigma-1}\left(I_{j,p,c}\right)^{\zeta-1}\left[\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}\lambda_{f,c}^{-1}\right]^{\varsigma}q_{j}c_{j,p}\\
= & \left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}-1}\left(\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho-1}\rho I_{j,p}^{-\rho-1}I_{j,c}^{\zeta\left(1/\varsigma-1\right)+\left(\varsigma+1\right)}\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}\lambda_{j,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}\left(\zeta_{j,p,c}d_{j,c}^{-\tau_{p}}\lambda_{f,c}^{-1}\right)^{-\frac{\varsigma^{2}}{1-\varsigma}\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}+\varsigma}q_{j}c_{j,p}\\
= & \left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}-1}\left(\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho-1}\rho I_{j,p}^{-\rho-1}\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}\lambda_{j,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}q_{j}c_{j,p}\\
I_{j,p}^{1+\rho+\rho\left(\varrho-1\right)}= & \left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A,k}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p,k}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}-1}\rho\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{j,c'}d_{j,c'}^{-\tau_{p}}\lambda_{j,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varsigma}{\varsigma}}\bar{q}_{j}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\\
I_{j,p}^{1+\rho\varrho}= & \left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A,k}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}-1}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{q}_{j}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\\
I_{j,p}= & \left(c_{j}^{1-\varrho}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{q}_{j}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Now we can divide across components to get
\[
\frac{I_{j,p}}{I_{j,p'}}=\left(\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}.
\]
Now consider the unit cost, where we substitute our solution
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}c_{j} & =\left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}}\\
 & =\left(\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}\left(c_{j}^{1-\varrho}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}\bar{q}_{j}\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}\right)^{-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\left(c_{j}^{1-\varrho}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{q}_{j}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\right)^{-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}}\\
c_{j}^{1+\left(1-\varrho\right)\frac{\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}} & =\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}}\\
c_{j} & =\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\varrho}{\varrho}\frac{1}{1+\rho}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
which we can plug back in to get 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}I_{j,p}= & \left(\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\varrho}{\varrho}\frac{1}{1+\rho}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{-\frac{\rho\left(1-\varrho\right)}{1+\rho}+1}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}\\
= & \left(\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}\left(\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\varrho}{\varrho}\frac{1}{1+\rho}}\right)^{\frac{1-\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Now let's say we wanted to concentrated out prices, then we would
use
\[
\bar{q}_{j}=Dp_{j}^{-\sigma}=D\mu^{-\sigma}c_{j}^{-\sigma}
\]
which suggests
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}c_{j}= & \left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\varrho}{\varrho}\frac{1}{1+\rho}}\\
= & \left(\rho D\mu^{-\sigma}c_{j}^{-\sigma}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\varrho}{\varrho}\frac{1}{1+\rho}}\\
c_{j}^{\frac{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}{1+\rho}}= & \left(\rho D\mu^{-\sigma}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\varrho}{\varrho}\frac{1}{1+\rho}}\\
c_{j}= & \left(\rho D\mu^{-\sigma}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\varrho}{\varrho}\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}
\end{alignedat}
\]


\subsection{Some Additional Relevant Results}

{[}X{]} Do input demand characterize. 

\paragraph{Input Demand.}

We have (Derivation for input demand )
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}x_{j}= & q_{j}\frac{\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\theta}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}\\
= & q_{j}\left(c_{j}^{1-\varrho}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{q}_{j}\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\theta}\\
= & q_{j}^{1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(c_{j}^{1-\varrho}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\theta-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\\
= & q_{j}^{1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(c_{j}^{1-\varrho}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\theta-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\\
= & q_{j}^{1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(c_{j}\right)^{\left(1-\varrho\right)\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\theta-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\\
= & D^{1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\mu_{j}c_{j}\right)^{-\sigma\left(1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}\right)}\left(c_{j}\right)^{\left(1-\varrho\right)\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\theta-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\\
= & D^{1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\mu_{j}\right)^{-\sigma\left(1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}\right)}\left(c_{j}\right)^{\left(1-\varrho\right)\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}-\sigma\left(1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}\right)}\left(\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\theta-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\\
= & D^{1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\mu_{j}\right)^{-\sigma\left(1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}\right)}\left(c_{j}\right)^{\left(1-\varrho\right)\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}-\sigma\left(1+\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}\right)}\left(\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\bar{p}_{j,p}^{\theta-\frac{\rho\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
{[}X{]} Discuss terms

Now we also have 

\paragraph{Upstream Profits. }

We start with 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\sum_{c}r_{c}^{\zeta}I_{f,c}= & I_{f}\frac{\sum_{c}r_{c}^{\zeta}\left(\zeta_{f,c}r_{c}^{-1}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}}{\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{f,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varsigma}}}\\
= & I_{f}\frac{\sum_{c}r_{c}^{\zeta-\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\zeta_{f,c}^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}}{\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{f,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varsigma}}}\\
= & I_{f}\frac{\sum_{c}r_{c}^{\frac{-\varsigma^{2}}{1-\varsigma}}\zeta_{f,c}^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}}{\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}r_{f,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varsigma}}}\\
= & I_{f}\frac{\sum_{c}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}\lambda_{f,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}}{\left(\sum_{c'}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}\lambda_{f,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varsigma}}}\\
= & I_{f}\left(\sum_{c}\left[\zeta_{f,c'}\lambda_{f,c'}^{-\zeta}\right]^{\frac{\varsigma}{1-\varsigma}}\right)^{\frac{\varsigma-1}{\varsigma}}=\frac{I_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
So we can write 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\pi_{f}= & \sum_{j}q_{f,j}\left(p_{f,j}-\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}\right)-\frac{I_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}-\sum_{j}\frac{I_{f,j}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\\
= & \sum_{j}q_{f,j}\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}-\frac{I_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}-\sum_{j}\frac{I_{f,j}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\\
= & \sum_{j}q_{f,j}\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}-2\frac{I_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}\\
= & \sum_{j}q_{f,j}\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f}^{-\rho}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}-2\frac{I_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}\\
= & I_{f}\left(I_{f}^{-\rho-1}\sum_{j}q_{f,j}\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}-\frac{2}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}\right)\\
= & I_{f}\left(\frac{\sum_{j}q_{f,j}\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}}{\rho\bar{\zeta}_{f}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}^{-\varrho}\bar{q}_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}}-\frac{2}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}\right)\\
= & \frac{I_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}\left(\frac{\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)}{\rho}-2\right)\\
= & \sum_{j}\frac{I_{f,j}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\frac{\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)}{\rho}-2\right)
\end{alignedat}
\]


\paragraph{Downstream Profits. }

We start with 
\[
\frac{I_{j,p}}{I_{j,p'}}=\left(\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p'}\bar{c}_{j,p'}^{\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}\text{ and }\begin{alignedat}{1}c_{j} & =\left(\left(\frac{c_{j,A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}+\left(\sum_{p}\frac{c_{j,p}}{I_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{\varrho}}\end{alignedat}
\]
so we can write
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}+\sum_{p}\frac{I_{f,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,p}}= & \frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}\left(1+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,p}}\right)\\
= & \frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}\left(1+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{-\rho\varrho}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{-\rho\varrho}\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)\\
= & \frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}\frac{1}{\left(\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{-\rho\varrho}\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}}\left(\left(\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{-\rho\varrho}\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{-\rho\varrho}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)\\
= & \frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}\frac{1}{\left(\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{-\rho\varrho}\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}}\left(\left(\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{-\rho\varrho}\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{-\rho\varrho}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)\\
= & \frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)\\
= & \frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\frac{c_{j}}{\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}1+\rho}
\end{alignedat}
\]
where recall 
\[
c_{j}=\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}\left(\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}+\sum_{p}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\varrho}{\varrho}\frac{1}{1+\rho}}
\]
Now note we also have 
\[
I_{j,p}=\left(c_{j}^{1-\varrho}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,p}\bar{q}_{j}\bar{c}_{j,p}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}
\]
so we can write 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}+\sum_{p}\frac{I_{f,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,p}}= & \frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\frac{c_{j}}{\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}1+\rho}\\
= & \frac{\left(c_{j}^{1-\varrho}\rho\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}\bar{q}_{j}\bar{c}_{j,A}^{\varrho}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\left(\frac{c_{j}}{\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{-\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}1+\rho}\\
= & c_{j}^{\left(1-\varrho\right)\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}+\left(\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}1+\rho\right)}\left(\rho\bar{q}_{j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\varrho}+\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}1+\rho}\left(\frac{\bar{c}_{j,A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{j,A}^{\rho}}\right)^{\frac{\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}+\frac{-\varrho}{1+\rho\varrho}}\\
= & \rho c_{j}\bar{q}_{j}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Now we can also write 

\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\pi_{f}= & q_{j}\left(p_{j}-c_{j}\right)-\frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}-\sum_{p}\frac{I_{f,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,p}}\\
= & q_{j}\left(\mu_{j}-1\right)c_{j}-\frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}-\sum_{p}\frac{I_{f,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,p}}\\
= & q_{j}\left(\mu_{j}-1\right)c_{j}-\rho c_{j}\bar{q}_{j}\\
= & q_{j}\left(\mu_{j}-1-\rho\right)c_{j}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Or 
\[
\pi_{f}=\left(\frac{\left(\mu_{j}-1\right)}{\rho}-1\right)\left(\frac{I_{A}}{\bar{\zeta}_{A}}+\sum_{p}\frac{I_{f,p}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,p}}\right)
\]


\section{Input Contracting}

XXX{[}Alternate Version to Get Elastic Profits{]}

Now let's suppose the price is set by the buyer - OEM has much of
the bargaining power. The buyer offers a contract to $f$ that minimizes
its cost of inputs:
\[
\min_{p_{f,j}}y_{j}p_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\text{ s.t. }p_{f,j}>c_{f,j}\left(p_{f,j}\right)\text{ and \ensuremath{\pi_{f,j}>0}}
\]
where $y_{j}=\frac{x_{j}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}P_{j}$ and
\[
\pi_{f,j}=\frac{x_{j}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}P_{j}p_{f,j}^{-\theta}\left(p_{f,j}-\frac{c_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}\right)-r\left(I_{f,j}+\frac{I_{f,j}}{\sum_{j}I_{f,j}}I_{f}\right)
\]
which we can use X to write as 
\[
\pi_{f,j}=q_{f,j}\left(p_{f,j}-\frac{c_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}\right)-2r\left(I_{f,j}\right)
\]
Now when we are in the inelastic case, $\theta<1$, we have that $p_{f,j}^{1-\theta}$
is increasing in $p_{f,j}$. So in order to minimize it, we set the
price at the minimum amount - i.e. at the constraint. But, we still
need the supplier to be willing to produce for the buyer, so we also
require positive profits from servicing $j$. So we want to induce
the lower possible cost, and offer the price that still has the supplier
investing in this relationship.

Let's start with profits, the buyer wants to place the supplier at
the participation constraint. Why? Now take the derivative w.r.t.
$p_{f.j}$ using the envelope theorem ($I_{f,j}$ chosen optimally)
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\frac{d\pi_{f,j}}{dp_{f,j}}= & -y_{j}\theta\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{-\theta-1}\left(p_{f,j}-\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}\right)+y_{j}\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{-\theta}\\
\propto & -\theta\left(p_{f,j}-\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}\right)+p_{f,j}\\
= & \theta\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}+p_{f,j}\left(1-\theta\right)\\
> & 0
\end{alignedat}
\]
So, since the participation constrain is increasing in price, and
the buyer wants to decrease price, they want to place the supplier
at constraint.

We next manipulate the P.C. as follows: 
\[
\pi_{f,j}=q_{f,j}\left(p_{f,j}-\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}\right)-r2I_{f,j}=0\implies I_{f,j}^{1+\rho}=\frac{1}{2r}q_{f,j}\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)\frac{\bar{c}_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}}
\]
Now going back to our derivation for the optimal R\&D, we have 
\[
\rho c_{f,j}q_{f,j}I_{f}^{-\rho}\frac{1}{r}=I_{f,j}^{1+\rho}
\]
which we substitute above to get 
\[
\mu_{f,j}=1+2\rho.
\]


\section{Comparative Statics}

Start from
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}p_{f,j}= & \mu_{f,j}c_{f,j}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{-\varrho}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}\end{alignedat}
\]
Now also write
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\bar{\pi}_{f,j}= & q_{f,j}\left(1-\mu_{f,j}^{-1}\right)\left(p_{f,j}\right)\\
= & x_{f,j}\left(1-\mu_{f,j}^{-1}\right)\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{1-\theta}\\
= & x_{f,j}\left(1-\mu_{f,j}^{-1}\right)\mu_{f,j}^{\left(1-\theta\right)}c_{f,j}^{\left(1-\theta\right)}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{-\left(1-\theta\right)\varrho}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{-\left(1-\theta\right)\rho}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}\\
= & \left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)x_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}c_{f,j}^{\left(1-\theta\right)}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{-\left(1-\theta\right)\varrho}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{-\left(1-\theta\right)\rho}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}\\
= & \left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)\left(\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}^{-\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\bar{\zeta}_{f}\left(\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}^{-\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{-\left(1-\theta\right)\rho}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
And implies
\[
\bar{\pi}_{f}=\left(\mu_{f,j}-1\right)\bar{\zeta}_{f}^{\frac{-\left(1-\theta\right)\rho}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\left(\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}^{-\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)_{j}^{\frac{1+\left(\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}
\]
Now we can write {[}x difference relevant for $\rho$,$\varrho${]}
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\begin{alignedat}{1}\sum\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(p_{f,j}/\mu_{f,j}\bar{c}_{f,j}\right)^{-1/\varrho} & =\end{alignedat}
 & \sum_{j}\frac{1}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}\\
= & \sum_{j}\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}\\
= & \left(\sum_{j}\frac{\bar{\zeta}_{f}}{\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}}\left(\bar{\zeta}_{f,j}\bar{x}_{f,j}\mu_{f,j}^{-\theta}\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\varrho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{\frac{2+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}{1+\left(\rho+\varrho\right)\left(1-\theta\right)}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
Now we also have 
\[

\subsection{Upstream New Derivation}

The suppliers optimization problem is 
\[
\max_{I_{f},I_{f,j}}\sum_{j}\underbrace{\bar{\mu}\frac{x_{j}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}P_{j}^{-1}}_{y_{j}}\left(I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}\right)^{\theta-1}-r\left(I_{f}+I_{f,j}\right)
\]
where $\left(\mu-1\right)\mu^{-\theta}=\bar{\mu}$ and $P_{j}=\int_{k}\left(\mu I_{k}^{-\rho}I_{k,j}^{-\rho}\right)^{-\theta}$.
Which yields {[}Alternate Test (Switch){]}(Upstream allocation derivation:
) (Alternate with varying $\rho,\varrho$)
\[
\pi_{f}=\lambda_{4}\left(\sum_{j}\left(y_{j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}\right)^{\frac{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}{-1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}
\]
\[
p_{f,j}=\lambda_{5}\mu\bar{c}_{f,j}\left(\left(y_{j}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\left(y_{j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}\right)^{\frac{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\times2\rho-\rho}\right)^{-1}
\]
\[
I_{f}+\sum_{j}I_{f,j}=\lambda_{6}\pi_{f}
\]


\subsection{Downstream Firms}

Consumers have CES preferences:
\[
U=\left(\int_{0}^{N}x_{j}^{\frac{\sigma-1}{\sigma}}dj\right)^{\frac{\sigma}{\sigma-1}}
\]
The demand each firm is (Demand derivation: )
\[
x_{j}=\bar{D}\frac{\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}}{\underbrace{\int_{0}^{N}p_{j}^{1-\sigma}dj}_{\equiv P^{1-\sigma}}}=\underbrace{D}_{\bar{D}P^{\sigma-1}}\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}
\]
The firms problem is to choose prices as well as a level of innovation
in assembly or input technology
\[
\max_{p_{j},I_{j,A},I_{j,I}}D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\times\left(p_{j}-\frac{c_{A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}-\frac{c_{I}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}\right)-r\left(I_{j,A}+I_{j,I}\right)
\]
Which yields (Downstream allocation derivation: )
\[
\pi_{j}=\lambda_{1}D^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\left(c_{A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{\left(\rho+1\right)\left(1-\sigma\right)}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}
\]
\[
p_{j}=\lambda_{2}D^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\left(c_{A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{\rho+1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}
\]
\begin{equation}
I_{j,A}+I_{j,I}=\lambda_{3}\pi_{j}\label{eq: Downstream Innovation and Profits}
\end{equation}
\[
I_{j,I}=\frac{\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}{\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}\lambda_{3}\lambda_{1}\pi_{j}
\]
(Derivation for input demand )

\subsection{Upstream Firms}

The suppliers optimization problem is 
\[
\max_{I_{f},I_{f,j}}\sum_{j}q_{f,j}\left(p_{f,j}-\frac{c_{f,j}}{I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}\right)-r\left(I_{f}+\sum_{j}I_{f,j}\right)
\]
where $q_{f,j}=\frac{x_{j}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}P_{j}p_{f,j}^{-\theta}$.
Taking first-order conditions, we have (Upstream allocation derivation:
)
\[
I_{f}=\left(\sum_{j}\left(c_{f,j}q_{f,j}\frac{\rho}{r}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}\right)^{\frac{1+\rho}{1+2\rho}}
\]
and 
\[
I_{f,j}=\frac{\left(c_{f,j}q_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}}{\sum_{j}\left(c_{f,j}q_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}}I_{f}
\]
It is evident from the second that 
\[
\sum_{j}I_{f,j}=I_{f}.
\]

\subsection{Geography}

Suppose there were local knowledge spillovers: 
\[
I_{f}^{\rho}\Rightarrow K^{\omega}I_{f}^{\rho}d_{f}^{-\tau},\quad I_{f,j}^{\rho}\Rightarrow K^{\omega}I_{f,j}^{\rho}d_{f,j}^{-\tau},\quad I_{j,A}^{\rho}\Rightarrow K^{\omega}I_{j,A}^{\rho}d_{j,A}^{-\tau},\quad I_{j,I}^{\rho}\Rightarrow K^{\omega}I_{j,I}^{\rho}d_{j,I}^{-\tau}
\]
where
\[
K=\sum_{f}I_{f}+\sum_{f}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}+\sum_{j}I_{j,I}+\sum_{j}I_{j,A}.
\]
Modifying to expressions to accommodate these changes is straightforward.
We just replace $c_{A},c_{I}$ or $y_{j}$ as follows and collect
like terms: 
\[
c_{A}\Rightarrow c_{A}K^{-\omega}d_{I}^{\tau},c_{I}\Rightarrow c_{I}K^{-\omega}d_{I}^{\tau},y_{j}\Rightarrow y_{j}K^{2\omega\left(\theta-1\right)}d_{f}^{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}d_{f,j}^{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}
\]
But, we need to make particular modifications to $p_{j}$ since it
is now defined as $p_{f,j}=\mu K^{-2\omega}I_{f}^{-\rho}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}d_{f,j}^{\tau}d_{f}^{\tau}$.
We now get (Spillover Derivations ). 
\[
\pi_{j}=\lambda_{1}D^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}K^{\omega\frac{\left(\sigma-1\right)}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\left(c_{A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{\left(\rho+1\right)\left(1-\sigma\right)}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}
\]
\[
p_{j}=\lambda_{2}D^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}K^{\frac{-\omega}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\left(c_{A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{\rho+1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}
\]
\[
\pi_{f}=\lambda_{4}K^{\frac{2\omega\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f}^{\frac{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\left(y_{j}d_{f,j}^{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}\right)^{\frac{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}
\]
\[
p_{f,j}=\lambda_{5}\mu K^{\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f}^{\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{\tau}{1+\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\left(y_{j}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\left(y_{j}d_{f,j}^{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}\right)^{\frac{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\times2\rho-\rho}\right)^{-1}
\]
(x-tilde expression here) (w/ asymmetries QQQ) 

\subsection{Symmetric Equilibrium \label{subsec: Symmetric Equilibrium}}
\begin{assumption*}
Downstream and upstream firms are respectively homogenous - sharing
the same exogenous demand and production components. 
\end{assumption*}

\paragraph*{Downstream Quantities}

We need to discipline a few equilibrium objects:

\[
D=\bar{D}\left(\int_{0}^{N}p_{j}^{1-\sigma}dj\right)^{-1}=\bar{D}N^{-1}p_{j}^{\sigma-1}
\]
now delivers (Derivation imposing symmetry downstream )
\begin{equation}
p_{j}=\tilde{\lambda}_{2}\left(\bar{D}N^{-1}\right)^{-\rho}K^{-\omega}\left(\left(c_{A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\rho+1}\label{eq: Equilibrium : Downstream Prices}
\end{equation}
\begin{equation}
\pi_{j}=\lambda_{1}\left(\bar{D}N^{-1}\right)\left(\tilde{\lambda}_{2}\right)^{\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\label{eq: Equilibrium : Downstream Profits}
\end{equation}
(Derivation for demand for inputs ) 
\begin{equation}
\tilde{x}_{j}\equiv\frac{x_{j}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}K^{\omega}d_{j,I}^{-\tau}}=\bar{D}N^{-1}\frac{\left(c_{j,I}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}\left(d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}{\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{j,I}d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}\label{eq: Equilibrium : Upstream Demand}
\end{equation}
where
\begin{equation}
c_{I}=\gamma M^{-1/\theta}p_{f,j}\label{eq: Equilibrium : Input Price Index}
\end{equation}


\paragraph*{Upstream Quantities}

Where (Derivation imposing symmetry upstream )
\[
p_{f,j}=\lambda_{5}\mu K^{\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f}^{\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(y_{j}\right)^{\frac{-2\rho}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}
\]
which simplifies to 
\begin{equation}
p_{f,j}=\lambda_{5}\mu K^{\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}}d_{f}^{\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho}}\left(\bar{\mu}\tilde{x}_{j}M^{-1}\right)^{-\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}\left(N\right)^{-\left(\frac{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}\times2\rho-1\right)\frac{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}{1+2\rho}}\label{eq: Equilibrium : Upstream Prices}
\end{equation}
\begin{equation}
\pi_{f}=\lambda_{4}K^{\frac{\omega\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f}^{\frac{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\bar{\mu}\tilde{x}_{j}M^{-1}p_{f,j}^{\theta}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(N\right)^{\frac{1+\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\label{eq: Equilibrium : Upstream Profits}
\end{equation}
\begin{equation}
\pi_{f}^{\gamma_{f}}=Mf_{M}\quad\text{and}\quad\pi_{j}^{\gamma_{j}}=Nf_{N}\label{eq: Equilibrium : Entry}
\end{equation}


\section{Theory Model Comparative Statics}

\subsection{Proving Lemma 1\label{subsec: Lemma : No Downstream Entry}}

Using the entry condition in (\ref{eq: Equilibrium : Entry}) and
downstream profit expression in \ref{eq: Equilibrium : Downstream Profits},
we can write 
\[
\left(\lambda_{1}\left(\bar{D}N^{-1}\right)\left(\tilde{\lambda}_{2}\right)^{\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\right)^{\gamma_{j}}=Nf_{N},
\]
where rearranging terms yields 
\[
N^{1+\gamma_{j}}=\left(\lambda_{1}\left(\bar{D}\right)\left(\tilde{\lambda}_{2}\right)^{\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\right)^{\gamma_{j}}f_{N}.
\]
This equation pins down the number of upstream firms as a function
of parameters - none of which we vary in the following comparative
statics. Therefore, we use the following result throughout the remaining
derivations: 
\[
d\log N=d\log\pi_{j}=d\log I_{j}=0.
\]
The intuition behind this Lemma. With a fixed markup and a fixed budget
of consumers, the profits extracted from the downstream sector are
fixed. An increase in the number of firms simply divides up that surplus
among a created number of firms. Since firm profits are strictly decreasing
in $N$ and firm entry costs are strictly increasing in $N$ - a unique
number of entrants is determined - regardless of other model features. 

One way to break this is to have substitutability with an outside
sector - creating a market expansion effect from lower costs: 
\[
D=\bar{D}P^{\sigma-1-\gamma}
\]
\[
\pi_{j}=\lambda_{1}\tilde{\lambda}_{2}^{\frac{\sigma-1-\gamma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\bar{D}N^{-1}\right)^{\frac{1}{1-\gamma\rho}}K^{\frac{\gamma\omega}{1-\gamma\rho}\left[1-\sigma+\frac{\sigma-1-\gamma}{1-\gamma\rho}\right]}\left(\left(c_{A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\left(\rho+1\right)\left[\frac{-\gamma}{1-\gamma\rho}\right]}
\]
\[
p_{j}=\tilde{\lambda}_{2}\left(\bar{D}N^{-1}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{1-\gamma\rho}}K^{\frac{-\omega}{1-\gamma\rho}}\left(\left(c_{A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{\rho+1}{1-\gamma\rho}}
\]
\[
\frac{x_{j}}{K^{\omega}I_{j,I}^{\rho}d_{j,I}^{-\tau}}=\frac{\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}}{\left(\left(c_{A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{1+\gamma}{1-\gamma\rho}}}\frac{1}{d_{j,I}^{-\tau}}
\]
an outside sector. A change in input prices or assembly costs does
not change how much revenue is generated by this industry. 

\subsection{Proving Lemma 2}

We have 
\[
K=\sum_{f}I_{f}+\sum_{f}\sum_{j}I_{f,j}+\sum_{j}I_{j,I}+\sum_{j}I_{j,A},
\]
were we use the symmetry assumption in Section \ref{subsec: Symmetric Equilibrium},
and innovation equations (\ref{eq: Downstream Innovation and Profits})
and (\ref{eq: Upstream Innovation and Profits}), to write 
\[
K=\lambda_{6}M\pi_{f}+\lambda_{3}N\pi_{j}.
\]
\[
d\log K=\lambda_{U}\left(d\log\pi+d\log M\right)=\lambda_{U}\left(1+\gamma_{f}\right)d\log\pi
\]
where we use Lemma \ref{subsec: Lemma : No Downstream Entry} and
define 
\[
\lambda_{U}=\frac{MI_{f}}{MI_{f}+NI_{j}}.
\]
Now consider taking the ratio of X and X, raising the later the power
of $-1/2\rho$. 
\[
\frac{\pi_{f}}{\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2\rho}}}=\frac{\lambda_{4}K^{\frac{\omega\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f}^{\frac{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(y_{j}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}}{\left(\lambda_{5}\mu K^{\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f}^{\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(y_{j}\right)^{\frac{-2\rho}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2\rho}}},
\]
where collecting terms we have 

\[
\frac{\pi_{f}}{\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2\rho}}}=\lambda_{4,5}K^{-\frac{\omega}{\rho}}d_{f}^{\frac{\tau}{2\rho}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{\tau}{2\rho}}.
\]
This result helps us characterize many of the first-order effects
of structural changes. It says that profit changes are almost directly
related to changes in input prices - modulus difference in spillovers
and distances which have additional effects on prices. 

Now using $d\log\pi=\frac{d\log K}{\lambda_{U}\left(1+\gamma_{f}\right)}$
from $X$ and $d\log M=\gamma_{f}d\log\pi.$, we can then write
\[
\left(1+\left(1+\gamma_{f}\right)\lambda_{U}\frac{\omega}{\rho}\right)d\log\pi_{f}=-\frac{1}{2\rho}d\log p_{f,j}+\frac{\tau}{2\rho}d\log d_{f}+\frac{\tau}{2\rho}d\log d_{f,j}
\]
When $\frac{\omega}{\rho}$ gets larger, then the effect on prices
is larger than the effect on profits. And when $\left(1+\gamma_{f}\right)\lambda_{U}$
the role of spiollovers in determining profits is larger, so elasticity
with respect to prices becomes weaker. (Unpack this(

\subsection{Existence and Uniqueness}

To prove the existence of an equilibrium, we will express the equilibrium
condition as the intersection of two curves that depend only on the
input price $p_{f,j}$. To prove existence, we will show that they
curves necessarily intersect. And to prove uniqueness, we will show
that the derivatives of these curves are bounded such that only one
intersection can every occur. 

First, we start by noting that $N$ is fixed as a function of exogenous
parameters. Next, note that we can substitute X into X to write
\[
K=\lambda_{6}M\pi_{f}+\lambda_{3}N\pi_{j}=\lambda_{6}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}+\lambda_{3}.
\]
and note form Lemma X we have 
\[
\pi_{f}=\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2\rho}}\lambda_{4,5}K^{-\frac{\omega}{\rho}}d_{f}^{\frac{\tau}{2\rho}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{\tau}{2\rho}}.
\]
So we can write 
\[
\pi_{f}\left(\lambda_{6}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}+\lambda_{3}\right)^{\frac{\omega}{\rho}}=\lambda_{4}\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2\rho}}.
\]
Now note if $\pi_{f}\rightarrow\infty$, then 
\[
\pi_{f}\left(\lambda_{6}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}+\lambda_{3}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}\right)^{\frac{\omega}{\rho}}>\pi_{f}\left(\lambda_{6}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}+\lambda_{3}\right)^{\frac{\omega}{\rho}}\implies\pi_{f}\left(\lambda_{6}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}+\lambda_{3}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}\right)^{\frac{\omega}{\rho}}>\lambda_{4}\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2\rho}}.
\]
So we can write
\[
\pi_{f}\geq\lambda_{7}p^{-\frac{1}{2\rho\left(1+\frac{\omega}{\rho}\left(1+\gamma\right)\right)}}
\]
Now if we take $p\rightarrow0$, and $\pi\rightarrow\infty$, we have
a lower bound for $\pi$. And if we require an upper bound for $\pi$,
we have
\[
\pi_{f}\left(\lambda_{6}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}\right)^{\frac{\omega}{\rho}}<\pi_{f}\left(\lambda_{6}\pi_{f}^{1+\gamma}+\lambda_{3}\right)^{\frac{\omega}{\rho}}\implies\pi_{f}<\lambda_{8}\left(p_{f,j}\right)^{-\frac{1}{2\rho\left(1+\frac{\omega}{\rho}\left(1+\gamma\right)\right)}}.
\]
Now if we take $p\rightarrow\infty$, and $\pi\rightarrow0$, we have
a upper bound for $\pi$. 

Now consider the following expression, 
\[
p_{f,j}=\lambda_{9}K^{\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}}\left(\frac{\left(\pi_{f}^{-\gamma/\theta}p_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}}{\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(\gamma\pi_{f}^{-\gamma/\theta}p_{f,j}d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}\pi_{f}^{-\gamma_{f}}\right)^{-\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}
\]
where we use X and substitute for X. Now consider the two sides of
this expression, which we refer to as $f\left(x\right)$ and $g\left(x\right)$:
\[
f(x)=x\text{ and }g\left(x\right)=\lambda_{9}K\left(x\right)^{\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}}\left(\frac{\left(\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}}{\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(\gamma\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}xd_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma_{f}}\right)^{-\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}
\]

Now consider the limit as $p\rightarrow0$ or $x\rightarrow0$ . We
employ the lower bound for $\pi_{f}$ and write 
\[
\pi\left(x\right)=x^{-\frac{1}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}},\text{ and }K\left(x\right)=\pi\left(x\right)^{1+\gamma}.
\]
Now note that 
\[
\left(\frac{\left(\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}}{\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(\gamma\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}xd_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}\right)^{-\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}=\left(\left(\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}}\times\left(\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(\gamma\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}xd_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)\right)^{\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}
\]
We want $g\left(x\right)$ to go to $\infty$ as $x\rightarrow0$,
so to be conservative, we use a smaller term:
\[
\left(\left(\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}}\times\left(\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(\gamma\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)\right)^{\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}>\left(\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\left(\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}.
\]
Now in order for $g\left(x\right)\rightarrow\infty$ as $x\rightarrow0$,
we require that $x$ have a negative exponent. Collect terms, we require
\[
\left(\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\right)\left[\frac{-\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}\left(1+\frac{1}{\theta}\frac{\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)\right]+\frac{2\omega}{1+2\rho}\left(\frac{1+\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)<0
\]
To make the parametric restriction clear, consider $\omega=0$, then
we have
\[
\left(\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\right)\left[\underbrace{\frac{-\gamma}{2\rho}}_{\text{Business Stealling}}+\underbrace{\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}\left(1+\frac{1}{\theta}\frac{\gamma}{2\rho}\right)}_{\text{Market Expansion}}\right]<0.
\]
In the other case, we want $g\left(x\right)$ to go to 0 as $x\rightarrow\infty$,
so we consider a worse case where $g\left(x\right)$ is bigger than
we suggest, 
\[
\left(\left(\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}}\times\left(\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(\gamma\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)\right)^{\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}<\left(\left(\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)\right)^{\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}.
\]
Now in order for $g\left(x\right)\rightarrow0$ as $x\rightarrow\infty$,
we require that $x$ have a negative exponent. Collect terms, we require
\[
\left(\frac{2}{1+2\rho}\right)\left[\frac{-\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}+\left(1+\frac{1}{\theta}\frac{\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)\right]+\frac{2\omega}{1+2\rho}\left(\frac{1+\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)<0
\]
or 
\[
\left(\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\right)\left[\underbrace{\frac{-\gamma}{2\rho}}_{\text{Business Stealling}}+\underbrace{\left(1+\frac{1}{\theta}\frac{\gamma}{2\rho}\right)}_{\text{Market Expansion}}\right]<0.
\]
Which is a stricter req. 

So we know they interesect at some point where $x=g(x)$. Now just
need to show that the elasticity is lower 

Now consider 
\[
f(x)=x\text{ and }g\left(x\right)=\lambda_{9}K\left(x\right)^{\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}}\left(\frac{\left(\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}x\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}}{\left(c_{j,A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(\gamma\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma/\theta}xd_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}\pi\left(x\right)^{-\gamma_{f}}\right)^{-\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}}
\]
\[
d\log g\left(x\right)=\left(\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}\left(1+\gamma\right)-\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left(-\gamma+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}\frac{\gamma}{\theta}+\lambda_{I}\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\gamma}{\theta}\right)\right)\left(\frac{-1}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)-\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left(-\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}-\lambda_{I}\frac{1}{\rho+1}\right)
\]
Now we want to to be $<1$. Let's maximize negative terms, make $\lambda_{I}=1$.
Then we have
\[
\left(\frac{2\omega}{1+2\rho}\left(1+\gamma\right)+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left(-\gamma+\frac{\gamma}{\theta}\right)\right)\left(\frac{1}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}
\]
Now suppose $\omega=0$. Then we have 
\[
\left(\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left(-\gamma+\frac{\gamma}{\theta}\right)\right)\left(\frac{1}{2\rho}\right)+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}<1
\]
\[
\left(\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left(\frac{-\gamma}{2\rho}+1+\frac{1}{\theta}\frac{\gamma}{2\rho}\right)\right)<1
\]
And we have 
\[
\left(\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\right)\left[\underbrace{\frac{-\gamma}{2\rho}}_{\text{Business Stealling}}+\underbrace{\left(1+\frac{1}{\theta}\frac{\gamma}{2\rho}\right)}_{\text{Market Expansion}}\right]<0.
\]
So existence guarantees, uniquness? Nice. 

But when $\omega\neq0$. 
\[
d\log g\left(x\right)=\left(\frac{2\omega}{1+2\rho}\left(1+\gamma\right)+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left(-\gamma+\frac{\gamma}{\theta}\right)\right)\left(\frac{1}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}
\]
\[
\left(\frac{2}{1+2\rho}\right)\left[\frac{-\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}+\left(1+\frac{1}{\theta}\frac{\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)\right]+\frac{2\omega}{1+2\rho}\left(\frac{1+\gamma}{2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)}\right)<0
\]
Which turns into $\sigma_{\pi,p}=1/2\rho\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\frac{\omega}{p}+1\right)$
\[
\frac{2\omega}{1+2\rho}\left(\left(1+\gamma\right)\sigma_{\pi,p}\right)+\left(\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\right)\left[-\gamma\sigma_{\pi,p}+\left(1+\frac{1}{\theta}\gamma\sigma_{\pi,p}\right)\right]<0
\]
and before we have 
\[
\left(\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\right)\left[\underbrace{\frac{-\gamma}{2\rho}}_{\text{Business Stealling}}+\underbrace{\left(1+\frac{1}{\theta}\frac{\gamma}{2\rho}\right)}_{\text{Market Expansion}}\right]<0.
\]


\subsection{Proving Proposition - Social Planner }

{[}Planning with heterogeneity {]} {[}National Competition with heterogeneity
{]}

Recall the optimization problems for downstream and upstream firms

\[
\max_{p_{j},I_{j,A},I_{j,I}}x_{j}\times\left(p_{j}-\frac{c_{A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}-\frac{c_{I}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}\right)-r\left(I_{j,A}+I_{j,I}\right)
\]
and
\[
\max_{I_{f},I_{f,j}}\sum_{j}\underbrace{\bar{\mu}\frac{x_{j}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}P_{j}^{-1}}_{y_{j}}\left(I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}\right)^{\theta-1}-r\left(I_{f}+\sum_{j}I_{f,j}\right)
\]
where
\[
x_{j}=D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma},c_{j,I}^{-1}=\gamma\left(P_{j}\right)^{-\frac{1}{\theta}},\text{ and }P_{j}=\sum p_{f,j}^{-\theta}.
\]

\paragraph{Planner Allocation}

{[}XXX With spillovers {]}

XXX Need to do something about the marginal value of entry in either
case. 

Consider an industry planner which jointly maximizes objectives, against
a foreign sector with elasticity of substitution $\sigma$: 

XXX Fix this, should not impose homogeeneigy first

XXXX Is this a perfect complement problem?????? Look into this for
why we don't have a publication of efforts problem 
\[
\max_{p_{j},I_{j,A},I_{j,I},I_{f},I_{f,j}}D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\times\left(p_{j}-\frac{c_{A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}-\frac{M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}}c_{f}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}\left(\sum\left(I^{\rho}\right)^{-\theta}\right)^{\frac{-1}{\theta}}\right)-r\left(I_{j,A}+I_{j,I}\right)-rM\left(I_{f}+I_{f,j}\right)
\]
We can write four first-order conditions for R\&D: 
\[
D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}c_{A}I_{j,A}^{-\rho-1}=r
\]
\[
D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\frac{M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}}c_{f}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}I_{j,I}^{-1}=r
\]
\[
D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\frac{M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}}c_{f}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}I_{f}^{-1}=Mr
\]
\[
D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\frac{M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}}c_{f}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}I_{f}^{\rho}I_{f,j}^{\rho}}I_{f,j}^{-1}=Mr
\]
from which we get $I_{f,j}=I_{f}=I_{j,I}/M$. Plugging back into the
last first-order condition, we get 
\[
D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}}c_{f}I_{f,j}^{-3\rho-1}M^{-\rho}=Mr.
\]
Now dividing the 1st by the above equation, we get 
\[
\frac{c_{A}I_{j,A}^{-\rho-1}}{M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}}c_{f}I_{f,j}^{-3\rho-1}M^{-\rho}}=\frac{1}{M}\implies M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}-\rho-1}\frac{c_{f}}{c_{A}}=\frac{I_{f,j}^{3\rho+1}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho+1}}
\]
or we can write the second FOC as 
\[
D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}}c_{f}M^{2\rho}I_{j,I}^{-3\rho-1}=r
\]
which in turn yields
\[
\frac{c_{A}I_{j,A}^{-\rho-1}}{M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}}c_{f}M^{2\rho}I_{j,I}^{-3\rho-1}}=1\implies M^{-\frac{1}{\theta}+2\rho}\frac{c_{f}}{c_{A}}=\frac{I_{f,I}^{3\rho+1}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho+1}}.
\]


\paragraph{Market Allocation}

Now as fragmented firms, we would have two first-order conditions
for downstream firms
\[
\left\{ I_{j,A}\right\} :\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}c_{A}I_{j,A}^{-\rho-1}=r
\]
\[
\left\{ I_{j,I}\right\} :\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}c_{I}I_{j,I}^{-\rho-1}=r
\]
and two more for upstream firms
\[
\left\{ I_{f}\right\} :\rho\left(\theta-1\right)I_{f}^{\rho\left(\theta-1\right)-1}y_{j}\left(I_{f,j}^{\rho}\right)^{\theta-1}=r
\]
\[
\left\{ I_{f,j}\right\} :\rho\left(\theta-1\right)I_{f,j}^{\rho\left(\theta-1\right)-1}y_{j}\left(I_{f}^{\rho}\right)^{\theta-1}=r
\]
It is clear from these laster two that $I_{f,j}=I_{f}$, hence we
can write
\[
\rho\left(\theta-1\right)I_{f}^{2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)-1}y_{j}=r.
\]
Now we define $y_{j}$ as 
\[
y_{j}=\bar{c}_{f}^{1-\theta}\bar{\mu}\frac{x_{j}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}P_{j}^{-1}=\bar{\mu}\frac{D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}M^{-1}p_{f}^{\theta}=\bar{c}_{f}^{1-\theta}\bar{\mu}\frac{D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}M^{-1}\left(\frac{\mu}{I_{f}^{2\rho}}\right)^{\theta}
\]
And we use the definition for $\bar{\mu}$ to get
\[
\rho\left(\theta-1\right)\left(\mu-1\right)\bar{c}_{f}^{1-\theta}I_{f}^{-2\rho-1}\frac{D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}M^{-1}=r.
\]
Now we have from downstream firms, we can rearrange terms to get 
\[
I_{j,I}^{\rho}=\left(\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}c_{I}\frac{1}{r}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}=\left(\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}M^{-1/\theta}\frac{\mu\bar{c}_{f}}{I_{f}^{2\rho}}\frac{1}{r}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}.
\]
Now we substitute these above to get 
\[
\rho\left(\theta-1\right)\left(\mu-1\right)\bar{c}_{f}I_{f}^{-2\rho-1}\frac{D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}}{\left(\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}M^{-1/\theta}\frac{\mu\bar{c}_{f}}{I_{f}^{2\rho}}\frac{1}{r}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}}M^{-1}=r
\]
which reduces into
\[
\left(\theta-1\right)\left(\mu-1\right)I_{f}^{-2\rho\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\right)-1}\frac{\left(\bar{c}_{f}\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\frac{1}{r}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}}{\left(\mu M^{-1/\theta}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}}M^{-1}=1.
\]
Now we have from downstream firms from the first first-order condition
\[
\left(\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\frac{1}{r}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}=I_{j,A}c_{A}^{-\frac{1}{1+\rho}},
\]
which we plug in above to get
\[
\left(\theta-1\right)\left(\mu-1\right)I_{f}^{-2\rho\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\right)-1}\frac{\left(\bar{c}_{f}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}I_{j,A}c_{A}^{-\frac{1}{1+\rho}}}{\left(\mu M^{-1/\theta}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}}M^{-1}=1
\]
which we rearrange to get
\[
M^{-1-\rho+\rho\frac{1}{\theta}}\frac{\bar{c}_{f}}{\bar{c}_{A}}\left(\frac{\left(\theta-1\right)\left(\mu-1\right)}{\left(\mu\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}}\right)^{1+\rho}=\frac{I_{f}^{1+3\rho}}{I_{j,A}^{1+\rho}}.
\]
Or, alternatively, we have 
\[
\left(\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\frac{1}{r}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}=I_{j,I}c_{I}^{-\frac{1}{1+\rho}}=I_{j,I}\left(M^{-1/\theta}\frac{\mu\bar{c}_{f}}{I_{f}^{2\rho}}\right)^{-\frac{1}{1+\rho}},
\]
which we plug in above to get
\[
\left(\theta-1\right)\left(\mu-1\right)I_{f}^{-2\rho\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\right)-1}\frac{\left(\bar{c}_{f}\right)^{\frac{1}{1+\rho}}I_{j,I}\left(M^{-1/\theta}\frac{\mu\bar{c}_{f}}{I_{f}^{2\rho}}\right)^{-\frac{1}{1+\rho}}}{\left(\mu M^{-1/\theta}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1+\rho}}}M^{-1}=1.
\]
which we rearrange to get
\[
\frac{\left(\theta-1\right)\left(\mu-1\right)}{\mu}M^{1/\theta-1}=\frac{I_{f}}{I_{j,I}}.
\]


\paragraph{With Heterogeneity}

Consider again the planner's problem: 

\[
\max_{p_{j},I_{j,A},I_{j,I},I_{f},I_{f,j}}D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\times\left(p_{j}-\frac{c_{A}}{I_{j,A}^{\rho}}-\frac{\left(\sum\left(\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f}^{-\rho}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}\right)^{-\theta}\right)^{\frac{-1}{\theta}}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}\right)-r\left(I_{j,A}+I_{j,I}\right)-rM\left(I_{f}+I_{f,j}\right)
\]
which yields
\[
\rho D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}\frac{\left(\sum\left(\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f}^{-\rho}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}\right)^{-\theta}\right)^{\frac{-1}{\theta}}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}\frac{\left(\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f}^{-\rho}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}\right)^{-\theta}}{\left(\sum\left(\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f}^{-\rho}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}\right)^{-\theta}\right)}I_{f,j}^{-1}=Mr.
\]
This gives a relative allocation of 
\[
\frac{\left(\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f}^{-2\rho}\right)^{-\theta}I_{f}^{-1}}{\left(\bar{c}_{f',j}I_{f'}^{-2\rho}\right)^{-\theta}I_{f'}^{-1}}=1\implies\frac{\left(\bar{c}_{f',j}\right)^{\frac{\theta}{1-2\theta\rho}}}{\left(\bar{c}_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{\theta}{1-2\theta\rho}}}=\frac{I_{f}}{I_{f'}}
\]
And for the decentralized solution we have 
\[
\left\{ I_{f}\right\} :\rho\left(\theta-1\right)I_{f}^{\rho\left(\theta-1\right)-1}y_{j}\left(I_{f,j}^{\rho}\right)^{\theta-1}=r
\]
\[
\left\{ I_{f,j}\right\} :\rho\left(\theta-1\right)I_{f,j}^{\rho\left(\theta-1\right)-1}y_{j}\left(I_{f}^{\rho}\right)^{\theta-1}=r
\]
It is clear from these laster two that $I_{f,j}=I_{f}$, hence we
can write
\[
\rho\left(\theta-1\right)I_{f}^{2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)-1}y_{j}=r.
\]
Now we define $y_{j}$ as 
\[
y_{j}=\bar{c}_{f,j}^{1-\theta}\bar{\mu}\frac{D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}\sum\left(\bar{c}_{f,j}I_{f}^{-\rho}I_{f,j}^{-\rho}\right)^{\theta}.
\]
This gives a relative allocation of 
\[
\frac{I_{f}^{2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)-1}\bar{c}_{f}^{1-\theta}}{I_{f}^{2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)-1}\bar{c}_{f}^{1-\theta}}=1\implies\frac{\left(\bar{c}_{f',j}\right)^{\frac{\theta-1}{1-2\left(\theta-1\right)\rho}}}{\left(\bar{c}_{f,j}\right)^{\frac{\theta-1}{1-2\left(\theta-1\right)\rho}}}=\frac{I_{f}}{I_{f'}}.
\]


\subsection{Proving Proposition Network}

(Derivation for input demand ) 
\[
\frac{x_{j}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}}=D^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\lambda_{2}\left(\left(c_{A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(c_{I}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}
\]
Now consider a whole vector of shocks 
\[
\left\{ d\log c_{f},d\log c_{f'},d\log c_{f''},...\right\} 
\]
\[
d\log W=-\sum_{j}\beta_{j}d\log p_{j}
\]
Matrix notation maybe with $P_{j}$?
\[
d\log p_{j}=\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}d\log D+\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}d\log P_{j}
\]
\[
d\log D=\left(\sigma-1\right)\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}^{D}d\log p_{j}
\]
Combine these to get 
\[
d\log D=\left(\sigma-1\right)\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}^{D}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\left(-\frac{1}{\theta}d\log P_{j}\right)
\]
Now let's consider the other changes: 
\[
d\log P_{j}=-\theta\sum_{f}\lambda_{f}^{P_{j}}d\log p_{f,j}
\]
\[
p_{f,j}=-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}d\log y_{j}-\left(\frac{2\rho}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)\sum_{k}\lambda_{f,j}^{p}d\log y_{j}
\]
where
\[
d\log y_{j}=\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}d\log D-\left(1+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log P_{j}+d\log\bar{c}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log c_{A}
\]
Then we get 
\[
d\log y_{f,j}=\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}^{D}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}d\log P_{j}-\left(1+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log P_{j}-d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log c_{A}
\]
From which we get 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}p_{f,j}= & -\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}d\log y_{j}-\left(\frac{2\rho}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)\sum_{k}\lambda_{f,j}^{p}d\log y_{j}\\
= & -\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\left[\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}^{D}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}d\log P_{j}-\left(1+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log P_{j}-d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log c_{A}\right]\\
 & -\left(\frac{2\rho}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)\sum_{k}\lambda_{f,j}^{p}\left[\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}^{D}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}d\log P_{j}-\left(1+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log P_{j}-d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log c_{A}\right]\\
= & -\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\left[\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}^{D}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}d\log P_{j}-\left(1+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log P_{j}-d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log c_{A}\right]\\
 & -\left(\frac{2\rho}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}^{D}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}d\log P_{j}+\left(\frac{2\rho}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)\sum_{k}\lambda_{f,j}^{p}\left[\left(1+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log P_{j}-d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log c_{A}\right]\\
= & \underbrace{-\frac{\sigma-1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}_{\text{Demand Shifter on \ensuremath{\tilde{x}}}}\left(\underbrace{\frac{2\rho}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}_{\text{Common\ensuremath{\tilde{x}}Effect on Prices}}\right)\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}^{D}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}d\log P_{j}+\underbrace{\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}_{\text{\text{Reallocation Effect}}}\left(\underbrace{-\theta}_{\text{Direct Competition}}+\underbrace{\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}}_{\text{Rising Inputs Prices on Inputs Demand - Prices/Profits/Reallocation}}\right)d\log P_{j}+\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log c_{A}\\
 & +\underbrace{\left(\frac{2\rho}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)}_{\text{Common R\&D and Reallocation from Others}}\sum_{j}\lambda_{f,j}^{p}\left[\left(-\theta+\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log P_{j}+d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)d\log c_{A}\right]
\end{alignedat}
\]
\[
p_{f,j}=\underbrace{\delta_{1}\sum_{j}\underbrace{\lambda_{j}^{D}\lambda_{j}^{F}}_{j\text{ in Industry}}d\log P_{j}}_{\text{Common Industry Demand}}+\underbrace{\delta_{2}\underbrace{\tilde{\lambda}_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}}_{\text{Inputs in j}}d\log P_{j}}_{\text{Specific \ensuremath{j} Demand}}+\delta_{3}d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\delta_{A}\tilde{\lambda}_{j}^{c_{A}}d\log c_{j}+\delta_{5}\underbrace{\sum_{j}\underbrace{\lambda_{f,j}^{p}}_{j\text{ in }f}\tilde{\lambda}_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}d\log P_{j}}_{\text{Spillovers Exposed}}+\delta_{5}\sum_{j}\lambda_{f,j}^{p}\left(d\log\bar{c}_{f}+\delta_{A}\tilde{\lambda}_{j}^{c_{A}}d\log c_{j}\right)
\]


\subsection{Proving Second-Order Effects}

XXX Or we say 
\[
\frac{d\log p_{f,j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}=\psi_{i,j}
\]

We ask 
\[
\frac{d\log p_{f,j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}=-\delta_{1}\sum_{j}\left(\Lambda_{j}\lambda_{j,I}\right)\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}+\delta_{2}\left(\tilde{\lambda}_{j,I}\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}\right)+\delta_{3}\sum_{j}\lambda_{f,j}\left(\tilde{\lambda}_{j,I}\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}\right)
\]

Now we ask 
\[
\frac{d^{2}\log p_{f,j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}=-\delta_{1}\sum_{j}\left(\frac{d\log\Lambda_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}\Lambda_{j}\lambda_{j,I}+\frac{d\log\lambda_{j,I}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}\lambda_{j,I}\Lambda_{j}\right)\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}+\delta_{2}\tilde{\lambda}_{j,I}\frac{d\log\tilde{\lambda}_{j,I}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}+\delta_{3}\sum_{j}\lambda_{f,j}\frac{d\log\lambda_{f,j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}\left(\tilde{\lambda}_{j,I}\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}\right)+\delta_{3}\sum_{j}\lambda_{f,j}\tilde{\lambda}_{j,I}\frac{d\log\tilde{\lambda}_{j,I}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,j}}
\]
Then we start to derive? So we have 
\[
\lambda_{j,I}=\frac{\left(P_{j}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}{\left(P_{j}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(\bar{c}_{j,A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}}\implies\frac{d\log\lambda_{j,I}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}=\frac{1}{\rho+1}\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}-\frac{1}{\rho+1}\lambda_{j,I}\frac{d\log P_{j}}{d\log\bar{c}_{f,k}}

Let's say downstream firms offshore. This has the effect of $d_{j,A}\uparrow$

\[

\]
Then we have
\[
d\log p_{f,j}=\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}d\log K+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}d\log M+\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho}d\log d_{f,j}-\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}d\log\tilde{x}_{j}
\]
So we can write
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}d\log p_{f,j}= & -\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}d\log\tilde{x}_{j}+\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}d\log K+\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho}d\log d_{f,j}+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}d\log M\\
= & -\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left[\left(\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}-\frac{1}{\rho+1}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)\left[d\log p_{f,j}-\frac{1}{\theta}d\log M\right]-\left(\frac{1}{\rho+1}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)\right)\left(d\log c_{j,A}+\tau d\log d_{j,A}\right)\right]\\
 & +\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}d\log K+\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho}d\log d_{f,j}+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}d\log M\\
= & \frac{\tau}{1+2\rho}d\log d_{f,j}+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left(\frac{1}{\rho+1}\left(1-\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)\right)\left(d\log c_{j,A}+\tau d\log d_{j,A}\right)\\
 & -\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}\left[\left(\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}-\frac{1}{\rho+1}\lambda_{j}^{p_{j}^{F}}\right)\left[d\log p_{f,j}-\frac{1}{\theta}d\log M\right]\right]+\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho}d\log K+\frac{2\rho}{1+2\rho}d\log M
\end{alignedat}
\]

\[

= & \left(c_{j,I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\left(\left(c_{j,I}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{j,A}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}D^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}

\]
\[
p_{j}=\lambda_{2}D^{\frac{-\rho}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}K^{\frac{-\omega}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\left(c_{A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{\rho+1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}
\]
So we have
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}\tilde{x}_{j}=\frac{D\left(p_{j}\right)^{-\sigma}}{I_{j,I}^{\rho}} & =\left(c_{j,I}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}\times D^{1+\frac{\sigma\rho-\rho}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\left(c_{A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{\sigma\rho}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}-\frac{\sigma\left(\rho+1\right)}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\\
 & =\left(c_{j,I}\right)^{\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}}\times D^{\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}\left(\left(c_{A}d_{j,A}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}+\left(c_{I}d_{j,I}^{\tau}\right)^{\frac{1}{\rho+1}}\right)^{\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}}
\end{alignedat}
\]
And we have 
\[
p_{f,j}=\lambda_{5}\mu K^{\frac{-2\omega}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f}^{\frac{\tau}{1+2\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}d_{f,j}^{\frac{\tau}{1+\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}}\left(\left(y_{j}\right)^{\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}\left(\sum_{j}\left(y_{j}d_{f,j}^{-\tau\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)^{\frac{1}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}}\right)^{\frac{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\times2\rho-\rho}\right)^{-1}
\]
So to start we have 
\[
d\log p_{f,j}=-\left(\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}d\log y_{j}+\left(\frac{2\rho}{1-2\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\right)\sum_{j}\lambda_{j}d\log y_{j}\right)=\frac{\tau}{1+\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}d\log d_{f,j}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}d\log y_{j}
\]
where
\[
d\log y_{j}=d\log\tilde{x}_{j}+\theta d\log p_{f,j}
\]
where 
\[
d\log\tilde{x}_{j}=\frac{-\rho}{\rho+1}d\log p_{f,j}+\frac{1}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}d\log D+\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\frac{-\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\right)\left(\lambda^{I}d\log p_{f,j}+\lambda^{A}d\log c_{j}^{A}+\tau\lambda^{A}d\log d_{j,A}\right)
\]
and
\[
d\log D=\left(\sigma-1\right)\sum_{j}\Lambda_{j}d\log p_{j}=0
\]
So we get 
\[
\begin{alignedat}{1}d\log y_{j}= & d\log\tilde{x}_{j}+\theta d\log p_{f,j}\\
= & -\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\right)\left(\lambda^{A}d\log c_{j}^{A}+\tau\lambda^{A}d\log d_{j,A}\right)+\left(\theta-\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}-\lambda^{I}\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\right)\right)d\log p_{f,j}
\end{alignedat}
\]
So we get 
\[
d\log p_{f,j}=\frac{\tau}{1+\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}d\log d_{f,j}-\left(\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}d\log y_{j}\right)
\]
\[
d\log p_{f,j}=\frac{\tau}{1+\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}d\log d_{f,j}-\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\left[-\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\right)\left(\lambda^{A}d\log c_{j}^{A}+\tau\lambda^{A}d\log d_{j,A}\right)+\left(\theta-\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}-\lambda^{I}\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\right)\right)d\log p_{f,j}\right]
\]
or
\[
d\log p_{f,j}=\frac{\frac{\tau}{1+\rho\left(1-\theta\right)}d\log d_{f,j}+\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\right)\left(\lambda^{A}d\log c_{j}^{A}+\tau\lambda^{A}d\log d_{j,A}\right)}{1+\frac{\rho}{1-\rho\left(\theta-1\right)}\left(\theta-\frac{\rho}{\rho+1}-\lambda^{I}\left(\frac{1}{1+\rho}\frac{\sigma}{1+\rho\left(1-\sigma\right)}\right)\right)}
\]
(w/ THIS IS THE ONE. asymmetries - assuming small firm)

\]
